target.name: CubeFabs
target.founder: Matthew Putman (PhD, CEO; former Columbia materials science professor) and his father John Putman

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 10 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
CubeFabs is a Series E semiconductor company founded by Matthew Putman (PhD, CEO; former Columbia materials science professor) and his father John Putman. The target has raised approximately $182M to date. The comparable universe comprises 11 peers across the Chips & Semiconductors sector: 3 public companies (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private companies, including Cerebras, SambaNova Systems, PsiQuantum, Etched, and Tenstorrent.

Traditional peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. The narrowly-filtered peer set—same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier—yielded fewer than three datapoints, falling below the minimum threshold for meaningful triangulation. While 10 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, none satisfy the stage and tier constraints simultaneously. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material bias and misrepresent CubeFabs's risk and capital intensity profile relative to semiconductor-specific comparables.

The absence of sufficient narrow-band comps reflects the heterogeneous nature of frontier semiconductor ventures. Without robust peer data meeting minimum sample criteria, this analysis cannot support a specific valuation range using quantitative benchmarking alone. A complete valuation framework would require supplementary methods (DCF, revenue/capital multiples, or bottom-up market sizing) paired with direct market intelligence on the identified private peer cohort. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation, path to commercialization, and capital requirements through cash-flow breakeven.
